commit.message:
Don't cast Diminish Spells on targets whose spells are already diminished

[Committer's notes: This commit is fixing how BEAM_DIMINISH_SPELLS
otherwise already has a proper antimagic check in it that was otherwise
being doubled-up to ignore the usual monster hex rules of not casting a
debuff spell on actors already bearing that debuff. This commit was part of
work being done on a future portal which contains another monster casting
Diminish Spells, and has been cherry-picked to master to both keep that
total commit batch cleaner alongside it hopefully further bumping up
currently somewhat low bes kemwar threat levels.]

Don't break player daze on the same turn it is applied

It already worked this way for monsters, but it was possible for the player
to become dazed and then have the daze broken by damage on the same turn,
resulting in the status effect doing literally nothing.

This should still be gentler than minipara since the player receives no
defense penalty during it.

[Committer's notes: This commit was part of work being done on a future
portal which contains a second dazing monster. It has been cherry-picked
to master to both keep that total commit batch cleaner alongside it
technically affecting zykzyl threat levels.]

Fix a Ru flesh redirection crash

Bolt of flesh was crashing when it didn't affect any actors,
which happened at least when Ru redirected the bolt to a
monster the caster can't actually hit.

Don't lost-soul-revive slowly dying monsters

In practice, this only affects unearthed wretches, who could previously
be revived by lost souls. This is both quite surprising as wretches
aren't really functional monsters, and the source of a rare crash from
nested deaths, where the wretches death caused their enchantments to
time out, causing them to die again within their death sequence.
Add friendly fire warning for Noxious Bog (#5154)

Add friendly fire warning for Noxious Bog (#5154)

commit.message: Allow autofight to fire through bushes (tekkud)
Clear out more accidentally-left-in debugging messages (cool 3)
Cut out some Trove debugging text (Undo)

Adjust default portal messages timing and prompts

Traditionally, on entering a level or reloading a save, all portal entries
have their force_more set on the flavour noise one can hear from them,
rather than the following line of the actual elucidation of which portal
that flavour line corresponds to. (The only exception to this is bazaars,
for some reason.) While it's nice to foreground the flavour line for each
portal as the first thing one reads, this has recurringly made it awkward
when there's anything ambushing the player on entering the floor, with the
exact naming and timer-explanation of a portal getting lost if there's a
sufficient number of lines afterwards, to the point of people searching up
the force_more flavour line rather than reading or finding the in-game
text itself on literally the very next line.

As such, this commit tweaks the force_more to appear with the "There is
an entrance to [wherever] on this level" line, rather than the individual
portal flavour announcement line. That line is still what reading
top-to-bottom will still see anyway in a short highlighted-yellow line, so
it should still function for flavour purposes being read before the more
gameplay-oriented matter. To help with noticing it in the message log,
this also comes with highlighting the entrance line in white.

Mini quick newer vaults(?) review

Worthy of note:

 * When troves were reworked to be theme-first in 3911a2f, the armour
   placements in each trove stopped using much of acquire armour, which
   innately had a very high chance for nagas and footaurs to get bardings
   (50% if they hadn't seen one yet, 25% after seeing each one). To
   correct this, troves matching various barding egos (fire and cold
   resistance in the Fire and Ice Troves, flying in the Air Trove, earth
   enchancement in the Earth Trove, stealth in the Hexes Trove) now have
   a ~25% chance per armour placement to place ego / randart bardings if
   one is a naga or gale centaur (though not for Sphinx Form characters,
   due to it being awkward to guess how long those characters want to even
   stay in such anyway). Alongside the Abyss-themed trove already using
   "acquire armour" itemspecs, this gives a ~23% chance for a naga or gale
   centaur to get at least one barding from a Trove (compared to the
   previous Trove chance of ~28% if one hasn't seen any bardings before
   and a ~21% chance otherwise).

   * (Bardings being an uncommon notable midgame power-spike as tangibly
      different texture from other slot drops is a feature for the
      barding-wearing species and not a bug, but Troves are in midgame
      and come with a lot of other upsides and cost downsides as is.)

 * The Xom bazaar also now has a 10% chance to contain a barding randart
   with a guaranteed downside of rF-, rC-, or Will-, because desperate
   players caring about an offering of  mixed upsides and downsides is a
   decent joke in-and-of-itself. (It probably doesn't mean a lot in the
   long course of a run, since Xom's random acquirements can roll bardings
   reasonably decently anyway, but anything more for people to pick and
   choose in the Xom bazaar is good texture.)

 * cheibrodos_lair_end_hotspot no longer has a slim chance for a
   mountainshell anymore, as the runed door made getting close too easy,
   and a more appropriate Lair end vault using the monster is slated for
   later in this version after another new Lair monster is added.

 * gammafunk_necropolis_ghost_cemetery no longer can place four or five
   ghosts pre-Lair, and has better rewards for the difficulty of a
   guaranteed two player ghosts at minimum.

Various tile replacements and edits, coldish undeadish edition

 * I've made new splinterfrost barricades tiles, compositing their
   previous use of DracoOmega's edits of ontoclasm's edits of Bloax's
   metal wall tiles with a CC0 icon by 7Soul1. This makes them look
   less like modified normal walls and much closer to the spell icon's
   emphasis of icy spiked sandwiched between metal, which even beyond
   the visual distinction helps emphasize they're not true walls for
   many other mechanical purposes.

 * After working on a future derived undead type for miscast reworks and
   Hell-Pan Roulette variety additions using spectral monster tiles, I
   have converted them back into the original type to help with quite a
   few missing spectral tiles, alongside a new texturing style so they
   come off as more of "ghostly shimmers" than the current "flat one-not
   monochromatic silhouette". (This also comes with a fair bit of bespoke
   choices even beyond the current other derived undead tiles, in part
   due the number of monster types that can't be normally zombified or
   skeletalized, and doesn't affect any of the orcs from Vanquished
   Vanguard due to orc tier being lost in this conversion otherwise.)

   Each of the current-extant ones uses the original spectral monster tile
   set ontoclasm made, while the additional ones mix them with other parts:
   * Scorpion spectres: Ontoclasm's ant spectre and lizard spectres.
   * Turtle spectres: Jpeg's ancient snapping turtle.
   * Constricting snake spectres: Roctavian's edit of ontoclasm's anaconda.
   * Bird spectres: Roctavian's original caustic shrike + zombie harpy
                    edit of ontoclasm's harpy tile.
   * Elephant spectres: Poor_Yurik's original elephant tile.
   * Winged quadruped spectres: Ontoclasm's quadruped and dragon spectres.
   * Iron dragon spectres: Ontoclasm's spectral dragon tile.
   * Quicksilver dragon spectres: Ontoclasm's black mamba + spectral
                                  dragon tiles.
   * Goblin spectres: Poor_Yurik's old goblin tile + ontoclasm's
                      generic small spectral tile.
   * Spriggan spectres: Poor_Yurik's old spriggan tiles.
   * Elf spectres: Sastreii's elf zombie tile + Denzi's old deep elf
                   annihilator tile.
   * Tengu spectres: roctavian's edits of totaku's original tengu tile
                     + ontoclasm's current generic small spectral tile.
   * Draconian spectres: Mitsuhiro Itakura's old draconian tiles.
   * Two-headed spectres: Ontoclasm's generic large and small spectres.
   * Elemental giant spectres: ontoclasm's generic large spectre +
                               roctavian's salamander flamebrand +
                               Co's old fire giant.
   * Juggernaut spectres: Ontoclasm's juggernaut and generic
                          large spectre tiles.
   * Winged humanoid spectres: Roctavian's original caustic shrike +
                               ontoclasm's bee and generic small spectres.
   * Holy winged humanoid spectres: ontoclasm's bat and generic
                                    small spectre tiles.
   * Minor demon spectres: Curio's ancient iron imp and ontoclasm's
                           generic small spectral tile.
   * Common demon spectres: roctavian's blue devil + Denzi's old smoke
                            demon and hairy devil + Ontoclasm's hydra
                            and generic small spectres.
   * Greater demon spectres: roctavian's unused balrug tile + ontoclasm's
                             generic large and small spectres.
   * Orb spectres: Sastreii's orb zombie and simulacrum.
   * Unseen horror / orb guardian spectres: Sastreii's X-shaped simulacrum.

 * A stray pixel on roctavian's current bog body tile has been fixed.

Don't revert lava beneath an active hellfire mortar (WizardIke)

This is entirely cosmetic now (and only possible to encounter with very
specific positioning), but it looks slightly odd, so seems reasonable to
avoid.

Don't trigger traps beneath non-moving monsters (WizardIke)

It turns out that 01c487f didn't entirely remove the behavior of dispersal
traps being instantly triggered when they reactivate beneath a monster.
This was because terrain changes call trigger_movement_effects() to handle
things like falling into water, and monsters (unlike players) could
trigger traps even without moving.

There are possibly a few other circumstances where it was previously
possible for a monster to retrigger a trap without moving, but I don't
really see a reason why they should act differently than the player in this
regard.

Move friendly(), neutral(), good_neutral() out of monster and into actor

This allows actors to be used with the SAME_ATTITUDE() macro, reducing
boilerplate in a few places. (And these methods already operated solely
on actor methods in the first place, and so don't even need to be
rewritten.)
Add Jewel to the credits

Bind right click to Describe when in Display Map to match Look Around

I kept getting thrown off when trying to right click examine things in
Display Map mode ('X') because it currently does nothing, requiring a
'v' press instead, so I matched the typical behavior of right click in
standard gameplay. Right clicks in map mode/`MOUSE_MODE_NORMAL` were
just being thrown away, so it's just using a button that was previously
unused.

I also moved describing into a seperate function with a position
argument instead of calling `process_command(CMD_MAP_DESCRIBE)` because
`CMD_MAP_DESCRIBE` requires you move the cursor first, which jarringly
moves the view as well, unlike the typical right click examine.

I agree with the comments about "this really seems like it shouldn't be
here" for the click handling code, but it's above my expertise so I
didn't do any further refactors; hopefully my change is small enough
that it's not growing the existing slightly-messy code too badly.

Bind right click to Describe when in Display Map to match Look Around

I kept getting thrown off when trying to right click examine things in
Display Map mode ('X') because it currently does nothing, requiring a
'v' press instead, so I matched the typical behavior of right click in
standard gameplay. Right clicks in map mode/`MOUSE_MODE_NORMAL` were
just being thrown away, so it's just using a button that was previously
unused.

I also moved describing into a seperate function with a position
argument instead of calling `process_command(CMD_MAP_DESCRIBE)` because
`CMD_MAP_DESCRIBE` requires you move the cursor first, which jarringly
moves the view as well, unlike the typical right click examine.

I agree with the comments about "this really seems like it shouldn't be
here" for the click handling code, but it's above my expertise so I
didn't do any further refactors; hopefully my change is small enough
that it's not growing the existing slightly-messy code too badly.

commit.message:
Handle force more after magic mapping better in console

If some terrain was magic mapped and then a force more happened before
the next call to viewwindow we wouldn't show the terrain as magic mapped
during the force more.

Update map correctly when Ashenzari reveals portals

19414c7 changed how we handle revelation, drawing mapped cells
immediately when revealed. However, it neglected to do this for
other methods of mapping, such as when Ashenzari reveals a portal.

We fix this so that whenever we set the magic mapping flag, we also
draw the mapped tile.

Add messages for firing without anything quivered

Fixes some code which intended to give a message when autofiring
without a quivered action, and adds the same message for CMD_FIRE.

Also tidy up quiver_description code to commonise a validity check,
and improve the consistency of how l-you.cc quiver functions work.

Fix Ru drink and words thresholds slightly

This makes them both obey their wording "below 50% of its maximum".

Previously, words allowed scrolls at exactly 50% HP, while drink banned
potions just below 50% if you had odd max HP.

Check skill training targets when cursing items

This isn't terribly important - without this we still check the target
next time we gain experience, and disable the skill then - but is a
bit clearer and is consistent with other strange skill gainings, like
draconian apts changes.
Show doom & contamination stats in the small layout

Paged inventory: Include Tab in the in-game help

Some Android users are confused with the new inventory interface. Arrow
keys are not available with the default virtual keyboard settings and
the in-game help does not mention the Tab key.
Fix inverted logic in is_temp_terrain (Dogchiho)

Don't generate smoke from temporary lava

Normally temporary lava (such as is generated by hellfire mortar) does not
produce smoke, but could be made to do so by saving and reloading the game
while this lava was active. (This is due to how the list of coordinates
used for smoke generation is populated upon level load. In fact, this will
cause any lava generated in *future* on those tiles to produce smoke, for
as long as the level remains in memory.)

This could be tactically useful in multiple scenarios (for instance, saving
and loading after being hit by Eruption could make it easier to flee from
a salamander tyrant.)

Now smoke should only generate from natural lava, even if the level is
reloaded.

Simplify hellfire mortar lava handling

Rather than using map markers to do reference counting on temporary lava
from hellfire mortars, and temporarily setting their timers to an infinite
duration, simply don't time out hellfire mortar terrain changes while the
matching mortar is alive. This should be functionally very similar, but
more concise and hopefully clearer.

(Also, I have given the mortar more duration upon being summoned, which
should improve the niche scenario where the player taking very slow actions
could cause the mortar to expire before firing its final shots. I don't
think there is any way this can cause it to exist for longer, in practice.)

Make map_markers more resiliant against dangling pointers

It was always possible in principle for Crawl code to delete a map_marker
while some other code was currently working on that same marker (as they
can be deleted from many places). For example, the previous version of
Malign Gateway markers could cause a marker deletion via a screen redraw,
which itself could be triggered in countless subtle ways.

In an attempt to mitigate these issues, map_markers::remove() no longer
immediately deletes a marker, but rather unlinks it from the map and flags
it for later deletion at the end of the next call of run_all() (which
happens once every turn). This should immediately stop other queries from
seeing this map marker, but not invalidate any transient references to it
that some other code might currently be holding. Since run_all() is called
in very narrow circumstances, this should hopefully be sufficient to
safeguard undefined behavior.

Slightly abbreviate some terrain change code

Now that get_makers_at() can ask for a specific sub-type.

Refactor handling of spike launchers, allow monsters to cast

Currently only on ghosts/illusions (the player spell's limitations are
slightly awkward for monsters to use, though the infrastructure can be used
for similar effects in future).

Add a map marker type for handling 'active' features

This is intended to be moderately general-purpose code to handle features
that should do something periodically of their own accord and need to
maintain some state.

It will be used in a subsequent commit to refactor spike launchers and
allow monsters to use them, but there are multiple future designs I have in
mind which could benefit from this. (I hope I have made their data members
sufficient to the task....)

Refactor malign gateway handling and tweak

The limitation of only one malign gateway existing per floor at once seems
to have possibly been because its implementation predated unique monster
IDs (!) and there was no way to unambiguously tell different casters apart
from each other, such that each would be properly limited to 1.

There is no reason this has to remain the case. And so I have rewritten a
bunch of its code to be a bit more modern. This has the following effects:

1) An arbitrary number of gateways may be opened at once (though still one
   per caster)
2) A gateway can now appear on 'floor-like' tiles besides plain floor and
   shallow water (such as lava or fountains) and will properly restore the
   original feature afterward.
3) A gateway will still produce some fog upon activating, even if the
   startup is short or the player is very slow.
4) The portal closing is now handled inside the map marker itself, rather
   than as ENCH_PORTAL_TIMER on the tentacle.

At the same time, I have made the startup of the portal slightly slower.
(Due to call order, the tentacle would frequently appear with zero delay
whatsoever, which felt needlessly similar to conventional summons. The
spell should remain very powerful even when slightly slower to start.)

Save compat code to properly upgrade old malign gateways seemed like it
would be quite messy for something which might affect literally zero
players ever, so I merely ignore old style markers. If this leaves someone
with a permanent (eventually hostile!) tentacle, so be it.

Refactor temporary terrain changes a little

It is now easier to end all terrain changes of a given type (and by a given
actor) with less boilerplate.

I also fixed how the mid of the source actor was confusingly named monnum
(even when it was the player) and was being marshalled as a short instead
of an int. In practice, I think it was hard for this to cause a real bug;
overflows were very plausible in real games, but having two actors that
incorrectly looked the same alive at the same time seems virtually
impossible. Still, it's good to use the actual proper data size.

Refactor the handling of several time-based effects that use map_markers

Several timed-based effects were handled by map markers (eg: Imprison,
Malign Gateway opening, Corruption monster spawning) and each of these had
their own bespoke function to iterate all map markers and apply per-tick
effects for any of a given type that they found.

This restructures handling of most such effects by introducing the
map_marker::run() method. When a marker is added to env.markers, if its
is_dynamic() method returns true, it is automatically added to a list that
will cause its run() method to be called every turn. This allows for future
time-based marker effects to be added without new boilerplate every time
(and very slightly reduces overhead in scanning for types of markers that
may or may not exist).

Temporary terrain transformations (otherwise the most common marker with
time-based effects) do *not* currently use this system, as code for
properly updating the player's LoS when they wear off requires all changes
to be done as a single batch (or walls reappearing near the player can
prevent knowledge of more distant changes that reverted at the same time).

Remove the need for markers.clear_need_active()

Only map_lua_marker ever does anything with activate(), yet most places
that used other markers needed to manually notify the marker manager every
time they added a marker that it didn't need to activation (and in some
cases, not doing so could cause an assert).

Instead, only set have_inactive_markers when adding a lua marker.

Remove code for reading/writing obsolete map markers

map_phoenix_marker and map_door_seal_marker have been obsolete and unused
since 2014, but their marshalling code (and much other boilerplate) was
left around so that saves containing these markers could still be loaded.

However, we were already marshalling the size of each map marker (even at
that point in time), so it is possible to simply 'skip over' them without
needing to keep so much dead code around.

Refactor to remove trap_def and trap_type

Internal handling of traps had a great deal of essentially vestigal code,
left over from when traps could be hidden, have finite ammunition, and
even share the same dungeon feature between multiple different types of
traps. Traps have had not had the need to track that kind of internal state
for a long time and needing to create a pair of feature and trap_def in
order for a trap to function needlessly complicated dealing with them.

Since every trap already had a dungeon_feature_type that unambiguously
mapped to a given trap_type, now having the proper feature is enough for
them to function. The last remaining use of trap 'ammo' was bespoke
handling for Passage of Golubria's remaining duration, but this can be
trivially handled by making it a temporary terrain transformation (PoG was
originally implemented before temporary terrain changes existed, and its
code was never changed).

Since traps are now placed like any other feature, the wizmode
trap-creation command is removed and vaults updated to use their feature
names instead of being slightly different than other features in that way.
The lua view.trap_at() function is also removed (as it is now entirely
redundant with view.feature_at() and all cases of ambiguous feature names
have been removed.)

This does mean that the strings used by c_trap_is_safe are now different
(eg: 'tyrant's trap' -> 'trap_tyrant'), but there should be very little
functional change outside of this. (I updated Sack of Spiders to be able
to web thing standing on top of pre-existing traps, though this was
already possible since 4169d62)

Don't immediately trigger traps that are created under entities

This was originally done so that inert dispersal traps would trigger as
soon as they recharged, if an entity was already standing on them.

I've been a little on the fence about this behavior. It makes some sense,
but also can result in allies walking on top of the traps (which they would
otherwise avoid if they were active) and then triggering them when they
reactivate. And since it would also have a mildly unfortunate interaction
with wizmode trap creation after the following commit, I am opting to
remove this behavior.

If this (rarely) encourages the player to stand on top of an inert
dispersal trap so that nothing can trigger it again, that is probably fine.

Change ^ from 'any random trap' to 'random depth-appropriate trap'

Almost no vaults use the default ^ behavior, since it is bad (placing D:1
Zot and alarm traps, branch-specific traps in any branch, etc.). But we
already have a function for 'depth-appropriate trap' (which otherwise is
inaccessible via vault definitions at all, as far as I can tell) and this
seems like far more sensible default behavior than 'any random trap in the
game, equally-weighted'.

Just imagine if 0s placed 'any random monster in the game'...

(Also, this will slightly simplify some future refactoring.)

Fix the lava from hellfire mortar not ending after changing levels

When changing levels unstable monsters such as hellfire mortars are
removed. This was done by calling reset on the monster, however, this
doesn't perform important cleanup such as removing the monster from the
mid cache or timing out lava from hellfire mortars. Instead call
monster_die with the KILL_RESET flag set to clean up the monster. Also
try to clean up permanent lava from old saves.

Fix crash on loading game with hellfire mortar active

The array that is used to read in a marker on loading a game was missing
the initialization for the entry for hellfire mortar's lava markers
causing a segfault when trying to call it.
fix a typo

Fix "Orcfreeti" showing magic bar in webtiles (code2828)

Webtiles was hiding the magic bar if your species name was "Djinni".
However, djinn of Beogh have there species name changed to "Orcfreeti"
which was causing them to show a magic bar. Fix this by checking the
species rather than its name.

Fixes #5077

Fix final effects being delayed for contam explosions (CrawlOdds)

If a contamination explosion scheduled a final effect (such as a final
effect to raise a spectre from a monster it kill when the player had
death channel active) the final effect wouldn't activate until after the
player's next turn.

Fixes #5133
fix: Make Naga/Salamander undead slither when animated (tekkud)
Fix a shadowed variable warning
Remove some unused variables from _regenerate_hp_and_mp
Fix some shadowed variable warnings
Fix unused parameter warning
Fix warning about using & instead of &&

Fix lava from hellfire mortar not lasting long enough (CrawlOdds)

In a turn, the duration remaining for temporary terrain is decreased by
how long the player's action took and then the monsters act. This means
that the temporary lava from hellfire mortar needs to last longer than
it takes the mortar to reach the end of the lava or the lava will
disappear just before the mortar acts. The lava disappearing under the
mortar will result in any traps under the lava reappearing and being
triggered by the mortar. To fix this, only start the lava's duration
ticking down after the mortar goes away.

Fixes #5128

Fix rare crashes involving Nobody/apostles being killed by polar vortex

If these monsters were killed by the damage from polar vortex while also
being held aloft on top of lethal terrain, timeout_enchantments() would
remove their flight and cause them to die in the *middle* of being revived,
resulting in a crash.

This (mildly hacky) change allows for specifically skipping the 'drown'
phase of removing ENCH_FLIGHT. Nobody blinks to safety afterward anyway,
and Beogh will specifically move apostles out of harm's way, if needed.

(This solution is not completely perfect, but the scenarios that still
result in a crash seem much more implausible and basicallly involve no
habitable terrain nearby at all.)

Fix apostles falling into lava during challenges not being noticed (Darby)

They would simply die without ending the challenge or allowing you to
recruit them.

This was because all monster revival effects would be ignored during
drowning deaths (besides Yred's Bind Soul). I am not sure this makes sense,
since multiple of these effects would work just fine in cases of drowning,
such as Blorkula bat splitting or Nobody's phyrric recollection (at worst,
the messaging might be slightly weird) and it's actually critical in the
case of apostles.

So let's not special-case drowning to avoid these effects (and in just a
couple places specifically exclude it, rather than universally).
Fix Tukima's Dance not working on draugr (Planckenstein)

A 0.34.1 changelog tweak

Mention a count of the other bugfixes not covered directly in the
changelog.

[skip ci]

(cherry picked from commit 1eebc1a2892e1c89776a0d7a10691f8dac8d9796)
Update trunk changelogs for 0.34 releases

Fixes and new functions for clua cell views

In 83290745, view.cell_see_cell() was simplified to directly check the
grid using the caching global LOS function cell_see_cell(). This is an
information leak, because it can check arbitrary grid positions (using
player-relative coordinates). This commit reworks this function to be
similar to the approach of its initial implementation in 306dcfe, which
uses only player map knowledge. It also adds
view.cell_see_cell_no_trans(), view.cell_see_cell_solid(), and
view.cell_see_cell_solid_see(), so clua has all necessary LOS checking
functions for arbitrary map squares.

It would be possible to implement these functions with the current
caching globallos cell_see_cell() function by expanding losfield_t to
two bytes and adding new LOS types. However this would slightly increase
crawl's memory consumption in all games for something that will only be
used in complicated clua scripting, hence I'm using the non-caching
approach.

Note: There may still be an information leak in these functions due to
use of map_bounds() with grid coordinates. This could allow clua to
check for map boundaries before the player knows this. However this is a
very minor leak, and I'd want to do some more investigating as to a
proper solution before reworking this aspect.

Revert "Fix monster extra action from torchlight being sometimes being delayed"

The approach used for automatically queuing monsters for action when they
gained sufficient energy was applying even to monsters outside of
env.mons[] (for instance, when the interlevel follower list was
unmarshalled). This meant that if an unmarshalled monster had sufficient
action energy, it could end up being used in handle_monsters() despite the
fact that the memory pointed to might not be valid anymore.

(A practical way to cause this in a real game is to have a fast monster
fall down a shaft, which can put it in the follower list while still having
action energy. It will get unmarshalled at game load, causing undefined
behavior to happen the next time the player acts.)

While the general idea is good in principle, I am reverting for now until
it can be implemented more safely (since multiple people's saves are
unloadable in the meantime).

This reverts bce5b7a

Change the glaive of Prune to the partisan of Prune

It's nice to have unrands for every weapon type, and well beyond the
alliteration, the trade of one base damage for the ability to wield a
shield or orb should be a reasonably worthwhile trade-off. (It still has
some very high competition amongst other polearm unrands, anyway, and
while it's a minor shame to lose another two-handed unrand of some
passable value, the upcoming two-handed weapons rebalancing project
planned for this version should help the other still-surviving ones.)

As such, this commit changes the glaive of Prune to the partisan of Prune.
This changes nothing about the actual stats or mechanics of the weapon,
so it's still a +12 polearm of freezing with mindelay at 20 skill that
provides damage reduction while one is paralyzed / petrified / asleep.
In becoming more attractive to more characters (and in being a freezing
weapon), it does however now get a 1/100 chance to place in the trove of
Ozocubu's Refidgerator.

(The tile modification here still uses ontoclasm's current glaive of Prune
item and player doll tiles, with the original versions of both now stored
in rltiles/UNUSED for potential future usage.)

New Xom action: mass vex + mass chaos lace

Unlike many other Xom effects geared specifically for variety's sake and
additional weird uniqueness, this new Xom action is also specifically
meant to help provide connectable Xom effects for a later-coming Xom
demonspawn priest in Pandemonium. (It'll just cast Vex, not Mass Vex.)
As such, it's a relatively straightforward effect: as long as there's any
hostile monsters in sight with attacks adjacent to another hostile monster,
this inflicts 4-8 turns of both Vex (the pseudo-paralysis effect standing
still and attacking things in place since f4f8fba) and Chaos Lace (the
chaos brand granting status effect from 4b73973, notably refactored in
3440744).

This may make multiple monsters shred each other and allow escape or
killing them alike, but characters reliant on melee don't get any
particularly large benefits outside of polearms (and vexed
polearms-wielders also have reaching anyway), and it's short enough
that monsters can survive briefly being made to hit each other. Involving
chaos brands still provides some immediate risk factors if one can't
escape and the rare chance to indirectly buff each other, but it should
still have enough benefits to be notable as another way Xom can interact
with fights without just directly heavily wrecking monsters immediately
or dropping down a bunch of allies.

New Xom action: mass blind + mass blinkitis

There has been some longterm dev discussion about adjusting and replacing
high-level Translocations spells over the past version's development,
which included replacing Disjunction. Whether or not this this ends up
happening, the design space for Xom to actually intervene in fights
positively besides killing enemies directly is pretty large yet mostly
only covered by teleports, terrain effects, or potions, so coming up with
something unique to Xom that can cover for the eventual fate of the spell
is a reasonable move either way.

This new rare "positive" effect requires one to be below 75% of their max
HP, to have at least one adjacent monster, and for there to be one or less
smiting-attack monster present for every five other monsters. If all of
these criteria are met, Xom blinds every hostile monster in sight
(regardless of their resistances, like how Enfeeble can since 589dcd5),
inflicts the dart of disjunction rapid-short-blinking-away effect from
d268329, and instantly blinks those affected that turn, damaging them
for 2d2 irresistable damage each turn and producing some translocational
energy clouds under their previous locations + your own square. (The exact
behaviour of this is a little unique, since it lasts much longer than the
dart- affected monsters just out of the edge of sight will be able to walk
back into view for one turn, and then be automataically blinked away again
when they'd next get to act.)

The blind should reasonably make up for pushing ranged enemies away from
a melee character (but doesn't completely negate them), while the
blinkitis prevents Short Blades characters from immediately stabbing such
irresistably weakened foes. What this _does_ do is allow a quick escape
(with the occasional ranged finishing-off option otherwise), with enough
of a melodramatic animation and visibly silly fashion it should hopefully
pass for a decent joke as well as a somewhat unique mechanical effect.

Fresh 0.35 Xom changes

Worthy of note:

 * After some refactoring to improve performance with floor exploration
   estimates, Xom's boredom is now much more pressing on mostly-explored
   floors (which span from estimates of 85+% cleared). While Xom's bored,
   the draining, Doom, and random mutations effects are amplified, and the
   random noise and flavour messages effects are skipped. Also, good
   mutation gifts + item gifts can't happen at all on nearly-completed
   floors, Xom's boredom timer now ticks down faster on nearly-completed
   floors, and said timer resets to a much lower count on such floors; the
   announcements of Xom getting bored are rescaled to apply to such
   circumstances, and Xom also announces the shortened boredom timer when
   on a sufficiently-explored floor. (All of these effects do not apply to
   the inconsistently-available Necropolis or Gauntlet floors, but does
   apply to Temple and Bazaars both. To offset this for normal play, Xom
   now doesn't tick down boredom on barely-explored floors).

   This repeated usage of floor estimates should continue to somewhat
   stymie Xomscumming with relatively minimal codebase burden or effort
   (while concentrated dev interests in revising Xom's strategic
   benefits distribution are pointed elsewhere), without actually making
   regular Xom play particularly worse off outside of backtracking for
   runed door vaults / shops / moving between branches. (One could, of
   course, scum on unexplored levels, but this amplifies the chance of
   teleport and noise effects being much more relevant instead, and
   those teleports slowly explore through more of the floor. One could
   also skip floors and return to them when they're effectively harmless,
   though this eats up Xom effect rolls with e.g. detection and allies.)

 * Xom's good mutation gift now more consistently has less chances
   happen the more one is already mutated, though with only minor lowered
   chances overall. Previously, this only started to have any effect at
   all when one had over 16 mutations, which was much higher than Jiyva or
   what general random mutation sources could ever really reach in the
   first place. With the Xom bazaar already covering for some of this,
   the

 * Xom's hyper-buffing-allies effect now includes chaos lace (extra chaos
   effects on attacks), paradox touched (learning Manifold Assault), and
   warding, to make them even more powerful at clearing fights but also
   notably less easy to just hide behind.

 * Plain shapeshifters have been nudged up a few effective levels in Xom's
   summons list, since they can high-roll some relatively awkwardly nasty
   things for early on.

 * Xom's tension spell list no longer includes Disjunction, due to a new
   effect replacing it in an upcoming commit.

New unrand: the swamp witch's dragon scales

For some strange reason, we have extremely few dragon scale unrands
overall, with just the faerie dragon scales and the scales of the Dragon
King at both far ends of armour weight. (Plausibly it's due to the
inherent unique nature of dragon armour as flavoured death drops, but
unrands are rare enough we can offer other sources of this anyway.)
Between trying to support somewhat heavier EV / casting armour as
occasional options and giving a little more for options for the three
limited-body-armour species, it'd be neat to add more of these overall.

These swamp witch's dragon scales play off of the flavour of the strangely
one-off presence of chimeric fenstrider witches in Swamp, and mechanically
links their debuff to their partially-resistable poison attacks. Aside
from being +5 swamp dragon scales of Int+3 with innate rPois, wearing them
gives access to a TrickPois mechanic that poisons enemies you debuff- the
maximum four levels of poison with rPois0 or rPois-, one level for rPois+,
and nothing for further than that.

Unlike the debuff-checking AC of poltergeists or mostly single-target
range of athame, the free AOE damage attached onto this (at least,
turn-economy-wise) also notably encourages some cute synergies with some
underperforming mass-debuff spells (Metabolic Englaciation, Alistair's
Intoxication), the ever-contested Uskayaw, and some recklessness with
scrolls of immolation every character can use, while not being quite as
inherently ally-hostile as OTR itself is. (It does anti-synergize with
sleepstabs from Ensorcelled Hibernation, but the one-turn sleep guarantee
there still allows stabbing immediately-adjacent targets.) As poison
itself eventually falls off at the end of 3-rune games, it eventually
mostly just serves functionally as +5 swamp dragon scales, which are fine.

(For the most part, we're fine with some major outliers of unrand power-
Arga, Gyre & Gimble, Damnation, and Cigotuvi's Embrace most immediately
come to mind, but plenty of others can be argued as quite exceptional
overall. These scales are quite strong earlygame, but their innately
conditional power should be fine enough to experiment with.)

The item tile combines Sastreii's current dragon scale tiles with
roctavian's current ego scaale mail tile, while the paper doll tile
combines Sastreii's current Kyria's mail coat paper doll tile with
ontoclasm's swamp dragon scales paper doll tile.

Nudge up athame drop rates and quality

While athame were meant to not significantly buff Short Blades overall,
they're quite rare at the point in the game where one may actually upgrade
from an elec / poison branded rapier-or-worse, which hurts their capacity
to actually help with the skill investment curve and weapon build
variation as was explained in 81124d8. (They're still a lot more common
than quick blades, but not by quite enough to make their specific demands
more worthwhile, and as with all weapons, plain base types that aren't
supposed to be endgame weapons are somewhat unimpressive post-Lair). As
such, this commit adjusts their drop rate and quality a bit:

 * Josephine and Nergalle both now have a 20% chance instead of a ~14.2%
   chance to be holding an athame instead of daggers- it's nice for
   uniques to have more rewards, anyway.

 * Orc sorcerers and kobold demonologists now have a slim chance for
   getting athames. With the above change, this increases the average
   athame count in D from ~0.25 to ~0.60 and adds another ~0.50 athames
   to Orc.

 * Deep elf death mages, sorcerers, and demonologists now have a
   40% chance for their athames to be flaming or freezing, as is
   mildly better for athames over other short blades.

 * Branded athames now have a chance for draining brand, as this only
   provides half of the athame bonus in a capacity that falls off in
   endgame, and it's nice for more characters to actually care about a
   brand with so many limitations and non-obvious mechanics attached to
   it.

Fix many cases of reaching full hp not attuning regen (yrdzrfxndfvh)

Items of health regeneration would only attune when you reached full
health from regeneration or max health being reduced. If you reached
full health from another source such as drinking a healing potion or
vampiric draining, the item wouldn't attune until you took damage and
then reached full health with one of the methods that works. Items of
magic regeneration had a similar problem and would only attune when your
magic regenerated to full. This was broken in commit 4e4bab2 as it
stopped checking every turn if items should be attuned and instead only
checked on some methods of reaching full health or magic.

Fixes #5080

Fix monster extra action from torchlight being sometimes being delayed

When a undead monster is hit by hurl torchlight it gains energy, which
often allows it to take another action. However, if the monster had
already taken all of its actions this round it wouldn't be added to the
queue of monsters waiting to take actions resulting in its extra action
being delayed until after the player's next turn.

A clua function to check if a square was seen

The function view.feature_at() can tell the user if the square's terrain
is known, either because it was seen or it was mapped by ?revelation,
but it can't indicate whether the square has actually been in player
LOS. Hence I'm adding view.seen_at(), which returns true if a square was
seen before. This function respects map forgetting via X->^F, so it will
return false for seen squares that were subsequently forgotten.

Refactor checking if final effects are mergeable

Check if the final effects are of the same type in the base class rather
than in every derived class. This should be less error prone when added
a new final effect.

Fix merging not taking the right amount of energy for slimes

After slime creatures merge, you should be able to walk 1 space away
from the resulting slime creature without it following you (At least if
you have normal move speed and the slime isn't hasted). However, our
implementation of this had the following problems

1. It was assuming that monsters take their turns in order of mindex
   (there was even a helpful commit saying this), however, this hasn't
   been true in a very long time. This was resulting in it not removing
   enough action energy for the player to move away from the merged
   slime when it thought it had had all its turns but it hadn't and
   removing more action energy than intended in the opposite situation.

2. The check to see if the merged slime was already low enough in energy
   was broken in commit d1e63fa and would result in a slime that was
   already low enough in energy actually gaining enough energy that the
   player couldn't walk away.

3. We were only removing 1 extra turn worth of action energy from the
   merged slime creature if we thought it hadn't had its turn yet,
   however, if the player's previous turn was slow it could have enough
   energy for multiple turns resulting in us not removing enough energy
   to let the player walk away.

To fix all these problems, set the merged slime's action energy to be
exactly enough for the player to move away with a normal move speed if
the slime isn't hasted.

Fix monster clockwork bee's action sometimes being delayed

In handle_monsters we iterate over all monsters calling
_pre_monster_move on each monster. If one of these monsters has finished
winding up its clockwork bee, this will add the newly created bee to the
monster array that we are iterating over. If the bee gets added before
where we currently are in the iteration order it won't be added to the
queue of monsters to take actions and its first action will be delayed
to next turn, however, if the bee is added after our current iteration
position it will be added to queue of monsters to take actions and will
take its first action this turn.

We can fix this be calling queue_monster_for_action on the bee when we
create it so that the bee always takes its first action this turn (this
will sometimes result in the bee being added to the queue twice, but
this is harmless).
Fix header build tests
A clua function for the Zot orb monster

Add a default force_more for a gale centaur's wind beginning to shift

(Due to training skills not corresponding to their current wind.)

I thought this was already the case, but apparently not.

Abort when casting Hoarfrost Cannonade with no valid space to place cannons

This fixes #5121

Don't make wizmode terrain creation default to targeting monsters

This closes #5112

Fix invisible monsters counterintuitively interrupting stampede

If the player expected to be making a rampage move (ie: that there wasn't
a monster adjacent to them), moving into an invisible monster would stop
their rampage dead in its tracks. Which honestly doesn't make very much
sense given that the entire idea is that you're bowling over everything in
your way, anyway. So now - unseen monster or no - you can keep on charging
forward.

Fix getting rampage attack stagger against adjacent targets

This was possible if the player *thought* they could rampage towards some
distant monster, but an adjacent invisible monster was actually in their
way. Thus you would try to rampage and hit the first monster encountered
with a rampage attack (and its stagger).

However, this used attack delay instead of movement delay, and could be
repeated. Meaning that in theory one could permanently stunlock an adjacent
invisible monster with a 5 aut weapon, so long as there was something
behind it to rampage towards. This might not have even been very hard to
set up in a corridor if one knew about it!

Now the stagger should only be applied if the player has *actually* moved
at least one step before attacking.

Don't have marionette continue to treat charmed monsters as hostile

If a Aphotic Marionette changed some monster's loyalty (eg: by charming
it), the marionetted monster would happily keep blasting away at it with
subsequent spells anyway. Now they should treat it as friendly as soon as
it becomes so.

Fix Dominate Undead not working properly for friendly casters

For instance, charmed bloodprinces (or more plausibly Marionetted ones).

It was applying ENCH_HEXED even to naturally hostile monsters, which didn't
actually do anything helpful (and also resulted in the odd situation where
the game would claim when you examined it that control over it had been
wrested from you, even though you never controlled it at any point).

Now it should properly charm undead to your side if the caster is friendly.

Fix damageless projectiles (like pyre arrow) saying they did not damage

Of course they didn't do damage. They never do damage.

(Broken by 22db19a)

Fix channelled spells not maintaining Stampede with East Wind

I'd accidentally put this code below most of the return statements. Oops.

Make Exposed dispellable

It feels like it's probably supposed to be.

Fix coglin ranged shadow mimics firing their second shot towards (0, 0)

Broken by 9780205

Fix Arena example syntax (canofworms)

'royal jelly' is the correct way to generate TRJ these days, not 'the
royal jelly'.

Don't claim Gloom has a 'chance to dazzle' in its targeter

Accidentally overlooked when it was changed from Dazzling Flash.

Add a wizmode command to add all generated unrands back to the pool

It's very inconvenient for testing certain monster interactions that one
can only give a monster a given unrand a single time per save file.

Fix unrand rarity reading undefined values

ec41f42 accidentally broke all unrand weights by neglecting to notice that
UNRAND_START must be subtracted from all unrand enums to index their data.
This meant that all unrands were reading their max item levels from past
the end of the array, resulting in undefined behavior. In practice, this
meant that early unrands would not respect item level at all, and some
random handful of unrands would be much rarer than they were supposed to
be.

(I suspect early unrand generation, even as intended, doesn't function very
well for other reasons, but this is undefined-behavior on top of those
issues.)

Make quicksilver elemental amorphous (Flugkiller)

In name allusion, text description and tile, quicksilver elementals are
formed out of liquid. This makes them just as valid for the Amorphous
flag as water elementals and wellsprings are when they got such in
cc540e3, which most notably makes them Shatter resistant but doesn't
do much else beyond adding to this consistency. Since there's already
28 caustic shrikes per Dis to provide a monster resistant to Shatter in a
branch full of those otherwise vulnerable to it, it should be fine to have
another monster resistant anyway.

Minature quick vault review

Of note, though also nearly everything done at all:

 * nicolae_elf_deep_mausoleum no longer uses curse skulls in Elf, as is
   pushing the branch guest capacities too far.

 * st_'s greater box V vaults set now properly places the golden metal
   tiles of 011df5c underneath its custom monster choices.

 * Slime:1-4 vaults with non-rock non-stair features now have an luniq
   tag. (I'd be happier to have more features and tiles that don't imply
   the slimes somehow missed wearing them down, but this should function
   for now.)

 * Lehudib's Moon Base's lunar statue has been adjusted to match other
   orange crystal statues aside from casting LCS over Mass Confusion.

 * Doroklohe's Tomb now has fairly better rewards than previously: the
   generic set has been removed, both item sets now have more gold,
   the scrolls set now comes with additional chance for parchments of
   earth, and the armour set has a variety of themed heavy armour or
   auxillary armour gear. As it is commonly regarded as a death trap
   (due to people not taking the digging hint to fight the boss enemies
   extra early), it might as well contain actually passable item rewards.

 * The Earth Ziggurat floor set no longer features caustic shrikes or
   entropy weavers, as that list has gotten quite large at this point, and
   we have enough sets emphasizing caustic shrikes at this point with the
   Acid Ziggurat floor set of e745ec4 joining the Lair, Dis, and Vestibule
   sets already mildly emphasizing these.

Small batch of (mostly Slime) vaults (#5010)

Adds a collection of Slime stairvaults, some themed and some generic,
all of which place monsters near the downstairs (or upstairs). With the
changes to slimy walls and Slime level connectivity, vaults can now
force players to go through nasty corridors without being seen as too
harsh. I don't think any of these are too mean, though.

Also includes one star-themed abyss monster vault, one abyss exit vault
based on a funny bug, and one ziggurat entrance with hostile statues.

Thanks to regret-index and gammafunk for layout advice.

[Committer's notes: alongside a few shifts of syntax and line length,
proper full randomization of stair uses, and a few tweaks of tags and
vault rewards, the notable changes were:

 * The Abyss exit vault riffing off of 830d415 was significantly revised,
   due to the bug existing not for very long in trunk as well as being
   far too opaque to actually parse out of context compared to e.g.
   `hangedman_abyss_monster_moat` still functioning as an abyssal blob of
   unexpected guest monsters.

 * Slime stair vaults containing monsters have gotten luniq tags with
   this impending batch, to mildly reduce the presence of multiple on a
   floor when there's still not a lot of Slime vault variation to work
   with.

 * The Ziggurat vault entry has a far lower weight than default, due to
   the statues used being mildly annoying and conspicuously more harmless
   and generic while being less representational than most other Ziggurat
   entry guard monsters, as well as the vault being a bit larger than the
   other Ziggurat entry vaults.

Closes #5010.]

Force Elf:2 to veto disconnected floors (various)

As has been reported as an Github issue many times, Elf:2's guaranteed Hall
of Blades can sometimes be disconnected from the rest of the level and
require digging or teleport to actually access. As it is a guaranteed
strategically-relevant (if still random) reward source, it's extra
awkward for the floor to be disconnected in any fashion. As 79647d7 reused
_add_connecting_escape_hatches to connect floors in Pan, this too reuses
the same to ensure Elf:2 will always be a fully connected floor, which
shouldn't add too much server loading burden with the individual case
but will address the problem otherwise quite directly.

This closes #3592, #4056, and #4720.

docs: changelog typos (Back-form) (#5076)

(Accidentally left off a fair bit past 0.34's release. Oh well.

Merges and closes #5076.)

Tweak starcursed mass messages versus mindless monsters (#4928)

As the comment left on that issue implies, we prefer the starcursed mass
scream affect all monsters with different messaging rather than make them
individually care about monster intelligence, so this commit does so.

Fixes #4928.

Invert default of autofight_no_stampede option and rename

While losing the stampede status when you need to keep it and stampeding
when you'd rather stay in place are perhaps similarly bad for the player,
using ctrl+dir to stop stampeding is more buttons than using tab (for a
very common situation) and it may not be immediately obvious to new players
that it even works. (Also, since for other species, tab only moves if you
*don't* attack, this is probably more consistent with core behavior
anyway.)

While this schema runs some risk of players instead not realizing they can
push monsters at all (which would be even worse), I am keeping my fingers
crosssed this isn't the case.

Move Gale Centaur to Advanced

Disagreements about their power level aside, multiple players have made
reasonable arguments that stampede presents a level of unique tactical
complexity that would be more appropriate for an Advanced species instead
of Intermediate.

I have swapped Coglins into the bottom of Intermediate in their place.

Rename Anemocentaur to Gale Centaur

While the mythological referent for anemocentaur were the Greek anemoi, I'd
opted to encorporate -centaur into the name (rather than simply call them
Anemoi) to try and ensure it was clear to players that they were basically
wind-themed centaurs and not anything more deliberately 'weird';
specifically harkening back to the familiar and oft-misssed species
rather than its multiple unconventional replacements.

This seems to have failed utterly, with many players from all corners
arriving at different conclusions about exactly what sort of weirdness the
name was intending. (The most common misunderstanding is that they are part
sea anemone, though I've seen a variety of distinct misreadings).

Since the entire *point* of calling them anemocentaurs in the first place
was to emphasise that they are, in fact, just centaurs (while being just
distinct enough to justify not being fast-moving like the common monster
is, on mechanical grounds), I am opting to rename them entirely. Hopefully
this will be less open to misunderstanding.

Output some crash logs on platforms that don't support unix signals

We can't output crash logs for sigfaults and other signals, but we can
still output them for calls to ASSERT and die

Fix the MSVC tiles build crashing when trying to process a crash

This resulted in the crash log not being created and was caused by
stderr being invalid. We can work around this by skipping writing to
stderr for this build configuration.

Fix crash when Bribing summoner (Undo)

When you see a summoner and its summons at the same time and the
summoner accepts a Gozag bribe (which kills the summons) we would crash
when trying to determine if the dead summons should interupt the player.
Instead, skip the dead monsters as they won't interupt the player.

Fixes #5115

Fix the force_scroll_targeter option messing with ability targeters

This seems to have been a copy-paste error when defining the option.

Fixes #5118

Fix unequipping a non-artifict always crashing

This was caused by commit a76044d which was checking if an item had an
artifact property without first checking if it was an artifact.
